description_long: This object is a rectangular guard stone made primarily of white marble, set on a slightly larger rectangular marble base and surrounded by a patch of green grass. The front face features a prominent reddish-brown metal plaque with an embossed image of a horse and rider, as well as inscribed text, suggesting a commemorative or historical function. The marble surfaces are smooth with subtle gray veining, and the edges are sharply defined, giving the object a solid, monolithic appearance. The rear and side faces are plain, lacking any decorative or functional features. The base is slightly weathered, with some discoloration and minor staining near the ground, likely from prolonged outdoor exposure. The overall form is robust and immovable, designed to serve as a protective or commemorative barrier in a public or historical urban setting.
description: A solid white marble guard stone with a reddish-brown metal plaque on the front, set on a marble base and surrounded by grass. The object is robust, commemorative, and features smooth, veined surfaces.
description_view_0: Front view showing the reddish-brown metal plaque with embossed imagery and text, set into the white marble face.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thickness of the marble block and the edge of the metal plaque, with plain marble surface.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a plain, unadorned marble surface with visible veining and no markings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the marble thickness and the edge of the plaque.
attribute_location: Typically placed at the entrance to historical sites, parks, or along urban sidewalks as a commemorative marker or protective barrier, often adjacent to pedestrian pathways or near building facades.
attribute_installation_orientation: Fully above ground, upright, and level with the surrounding surface, with the base resting on or slightly embedded in the ground.
attribute_wear_patterns: Minor discoloration and staining near the base from soil and moisture exposure, with possible slight smoothing of marble edges due to weathering. The metal plaque may show some patina or oxidation, especially around the edges and raised details.
attribute_historical_markings: A reddish-brown metal plaque on the front face features an embossed image of a horse and rider along with inscribed commemorative text.
